title: triaristella gemmata
intro:
Triaristella gemmata is a type of marine sponge, not a plant, however, I couldn't find any information on it.

However, I found information on Tripterygium wilfordii, also known as Thunder of God Vine, or more commonly referred to as Triaristela gemmata is not a known plant name but it may be referring to a misnomer for  Tripterygium wilfordii, a plant known for its medicinal properties. 

Tripterygium wilfordii has been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which can be beneficial for treating conditions such as arthritis and other autoimmune diseases. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or capsule for medicinal purposes, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects. 

Tripterygium wilfordii is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, and other autoimmune diseases, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
